description:
## Goal

The atomic move: typer, SpecRegistry, and codegen all switch off the
per-callsite model in a single commit. Intermediate state is
uncompilable, so this is one ticket. Regenerate 100 goldens. Measure
CLIF size delta and fixture_matrix wall delta.

Reads what fz-7ot.1 already populates.

## Typer changes (src/ir_typer.rs)

- Fold `FnTypes` into `Canonical` (one record per fn).
- `ModuleTypes::specs: HashMap<FnId, Canonical>` (was composite key).
- `ModuleTypes::effective_returns: HashMap<FnId, Descr>` (was composite).
- `entry_seeds` returns one seed per entry FnId — no any-key cohort.
- `walk_spec_for_discovery::push_key`: drop per-callsite mint; keep
  ONLY the LUB-merge + histogram bump introduced in fz-7ot.1.
- SCC + `widen()` at `WIDEN_AT=3` unchanged — same fixpoint machinery,
  same threshold, driving canonical LUB stability instead of per-spec.

## SpecRegistry changes (src/spec_registry.rs)

- Remove `by_fn: HashMap<FnId, Vec<SpecId>>` field.
- Remove subsumption tier in `resolve` (lines 99–149) — only the
  exact-match fast path remains.
- `resolve(fn_id, _input_descrs)`: input_descrs param retained for
  caller compat this pass (rip in fz-7ot.3).
- `register_any_key_at` is now the SOLE registration path; SpecId.0 ==
  FnId.0 invariant emerges naturally.

## Codegen changes (src/ir_codegen.rs)

DELETE:
- `tagged_return_specs` build, lines 2795–2867 (~72 lines).
- `tagged_return_fns` derivation, lines 2873–2880 (~8 lines).
- `tagged_slot0_cont_specs` + decision flow, lines 2891–2977 (~87 lines).
- 4 `naturally_opaque` reads at lines 1572, 1606, 2344, 2350: MakeClosure
  and CallClosure always wire canonical body. Conditional gates collapse.
- Comment references at lines 2614, 2693, 2747, 2777 — retired analyses.

REPLACE WITH:
- Per-slot ABI at every emit site: `ArgRepr::from_descr(&canonical[fn].slots[i].descr)`.
- Per-fn return ABI: `ArgRepr::from_descr(&canonical[fn].return_descr)`.

`reachable_specs` simplifies:
- Drop the MakeClosure-target blanket seed.
- Walk callee FnIds from annotated callsites; canonical of every
  transitively-reachable FnId is reachable. SpecId.0 == FnId.0
  invariant means the resulting set is FnId-aligned.

## What gets removed

- `fn ensure_any_keys` (src/ir_typer.rs:566).
- `ModuleTypes::naturally_opaque: HashSet<FnId>` + snapshot logic
  (lines 234, 517–521).
- `ModuleTypes::any_key_specs` (lines 217–225).
- Two-tier `SpecRegistry::resolve` subsumption fallback.
- `tagged_return_specs`, `tagged_return_fns`, `tagged_slot0_cont_specs`
  and their propagation loops.
- Per-callsite specs HashMap composite key throughout.
- ~64 references to retired bookkeeping across ir_typer + ir_codegen.

## Goldens

Regenerate all 50 fixtures (expected.clif + expected.specs = 100
files) in a single bless pass. Commit message must record:
- pre/post CLIF line totals (target: majority reduction)
- pre/post fixture_matrix debug wall time (target: ≤ 20s)
- pre/post total SpecId count across fixtures (target: ~= total FnId
  count)

## Acceptance

- All 618 unit + 166 fixture-matrix tests pass with regenerated goldens.
- Spec count per fixture ≈ FnId count.
- CLIF lines drop by majority across the fixture set.
- fixture_matrix debug wall ≤ 20s.
- No clippy warnings; fmt clean.
- Annotation arc (fz-aiz.3a–c) unchanged in interface — every annotated
  callsite still resolves to its canonical's SpecId.

## Honest tradeoffs (re-stated)

- `fib(0, _, 1)` loses its constant-fold spec; routes through
  canonical body's clause-0 pattern match. A few extra instructions,
  not a regression worth optimizing absent measurement.
- Multi-clause fns with one narrow caller per clause may pay one
  extra branch vs today's narrow-spec model. Targeted refinement
  follow-up if measurable.

## See also

- fz-7ot.1 — populates the canonical model this ticket consumes.
- fz-7ot.3 — diagnostic + close fz-t45 + sweep; blocks on this.
- fz-t45 — TailCallClosure ABI mismatch; dissolves structurally here.
title: fz-7ot.2: demolition — collapse to one spec per fn
